task_completion.description: Optimize TextFileReader.java to fix byte-by-byte reading
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for TextFileReader.java open in the IntelliJ editor. The code should be modified to use BufferedReader, Files.readAllLines(), Files.readString(), or similar efficient reading methods instead of reading byte-by-byte with string concatenation.

task_completion.description: Optimize WordCounter.java to fix O(n) lookups
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for WordCounter.java open in the IntelliJ editor. The code should be modified to use a HashSet or similar O(1) lookup data structure instead of ArrayList.contains() inside a loop.

task_completion.description: Optimize DuplicateDetector.java to fix O(n²) nested loops
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for DuplicateDetector.java open in the IntelliJ editor. The nested 'for' loops should be removed and replaced with a more efficient O(n) approach, such as using a HashSet to track seen items.

task_completion.description: Optimize ReportGenerator.java to fix string concatenation in loops
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ReportGenerator.java open in the IntelliJ editor. The code should be modified to use StringBuilder or StringBuffer instead of the '+=' operator for string concatenation inside loops.

task_completion.description: Optimize TopWordsFinder.java to fix bubble sort
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for TopWordsFinder.java open in the IntelliJ editor. The manual bubble sort (nested loops swapping elements) should be replaced with Collections.sort(), List.sort(), or a PriorityQueue.

task_completion.description: Ensure all unit tests pass after optimizations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Run/Debug tool window in IntelliJ showing green checkmarks indicating all tests passed, or a terminal window showing 'BUILD SUCCESS' after running 'mvn test'.

Look for a file named 'optimization_results.txt' open in the editor or its contents displayed in the terminal. It must be located in the project root and contain text describing the performance improvements made to the 5 files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the IntelliJ IDEA interface to edit the files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively using the IntelliJ IDEA editor to modify the Java files, rather than bypassing the IDE entirely with terminal commands like 'sed' or 'echo'.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the Java source code
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Java files were genuinely edited to improve performance, rather than the agent just creating the optimization_results.txt file without making any actual code changes.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely ran the unit tests
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for authentic test execution output in the IntelliJ Run window or Maven terminal output, ensuring the agent didn't just print a fake 'Tests passed' message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is a Java 17 Maven project located at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/text-analyzer. The specific performance anti-patterns to fix are: 1) TextFileReader.java reads byte-by-byte with string concatenation (needs BufferedReader/Files API). 2) WordCounter.java uses ArrayList.contains() (needs HashSet). 3) DuplicateDetector.java uses O(n²) nested loops (needs HashSet). 4) ReportGenerator.java uses String += in loops (needs StringBuilder). 5) TopWordsFinder.java uses bubble sort (needs Collections.sort or similar). All tests must pass, and changes must be documented in optimization_results.txt in the project root.
